steps.title: Locate the known points from the figure
steps.content: From the figure, $P$ is at $(1, 0)$ on the positive $x$-axis (at \$0°$) and $T$ is at $(-1, 0)$ on the negative $x$-axis (at \$180°$). The center $O$ is at the origin. Points $Q$ and $R$ lie on the circle, with $Q$ in the first quadrant and $R$ in the second quadrant.

steps.title: Find the position of Q using angle POQ
steps.content: Since $\angle POQ = 30°$ and $P$ is at \$0°$, point $Q$ is located \$30°$ counterclockwise from $P$. Therefore, $Q$ is at \$30°$ from the positive $x$-axis.

steps.title: Find the position of R using angle ROT
steps.content: Since $\angle ROT = 30°$ and $T$ is at \$180°$, and $R$ appears above $T$ in the second quadrant, point $R$ is located \$30°$ counterclockwise from $T$. Therefore, $R$ is at $180° - 30° = 150°$ from the positive $x$-axis.

steps.title: Calculate angle QOR and convert to radians
steps.content: The central angle $\angle QOR$ is the difference between their positions: $$\angle QOR = 150° - 30° = 120°$$ Converting to radians: $$120° \times \frac{\pi}{180°} = \frac{120\pi}{180} = \frac{2\pi}{3}$$ That matches choice C — C is correct.
